title: Suggestions006 Design Audit
goal: Audit the explorer against the structural, visual, and interaction requirements in suggestions006.md using a guided Playwright session with reproducible evidence.
intent: Judge whether the page now behaves like a coherent field-guide workspace with a dominant catalog-detail surface, a visible roadmap, and clear map-to-catalog-to-detail relationships.
reviewQuestions: Is the catalog-detail relationship unmistakably the main work area within a few seconds?
Does the roadmap feel visible, useful, and operational rather than decorative?
Does the page now read as one guided workspace instead of a stack of unrelated panels?
Do selection, stage context, and nearby alternatives make the detail pane feel connected to the larger learning landscape?
reminders: Do not stop at functional success; inspect hierarchy, visual weight, and reading flow in the surrounding frame.
When the map technically filters or selects, still check whether the page explains the relationship clearly enough to a human viewer.
If a surface is visible but still competes too loudly with the main workspace, record that as a real issue.
baseline: Run the stable acceptance suite first.
Use the screenshots to judge hierarchy, not only DOM state.
Record observations in product language tied to a screenshot and requirement.

steps.title: Audit the opening hierarchy
steps.intent: Check whether the page now establishes a concise orientation layer and a clearly dominant workspace.
steps.focus: Landing viewport hierarchy, quieter left rail, and recommendation compactness.
lookAround: Can I identify the main work area without interpreting the whole page?
Does the left rail feel quieter than the workspace?
Does the recommendation layer feel compact and decisive instead of repetitive?
reviewQuestions: Does this opening behave like orientation before work, rather than like a dashboard?
steps.observation: The first viewport should orient quickly and make the catalog-detail workspace feel like the main task surface.

steps.title: Audit the roadmap as a first-class surface
steps.intent: Check whether the roadmap is visible by default and reads like navigation infrastructure.
steps.focus: Roadmap visibility, stage context chips, and map legibility in the first meaningful viewport.
lookAround: Does the roadmap feel like part of the product's reasoning system rather than a detached module?
Do the stage chips and lane labels help orientation at a glance?
Would a new user notice the map immediately without hunting?
reviewQuestions: Does the map now earn its prominence?
steps.observation: The roadmap should be visible infrastructure, not hidden compliance.

steps.title: Audit map-to-catalog linkage
steps.intent: Verify that stage interaction from the roadmap changes the visible slice in a meaningful, predictable way.
steps.focus: Phase context filter from roadmap into the catalog and summary layer.
lookAround: Does the visible slice now read as a region of the larger map rather than an unrelated filtered list?
Does the active summary explain the filtered stage clearly enough?
Do the catalog section headers still align with the selected region conceptually?
reviewQuestions: Would I understand from the UI itself that the roadmap has narrowed the workspace?
steps.observation: Stage selection from the roadmap should visibly reshape the catalog and context without feeling surprising.

steps.title: Audit catalog selection clarity
steps.intent: Check whether the selected row is unmistakable and visually tied to the detail pane.
steps.focus: Selected-state contrast, catalog scannability, and row-level emphasis.
lookAround: Can I tell immediately which row is selected?
Does the catalog still feel like a curated route rather than a feed of repeated boxes?
Do metadata and action controls stay subordinate to title and editorial summary?
reviewQuestions: Would a fast scanner ever wonder which problem is active?
steps.observation: The selected row should lock visually to the detail pane and remain obvious during scanning.

steps.title: Audit the detail pane as a reading surface
steps.intent: Check whether the detail pane now reads like a calm inspection panel tied to the global structure.
steps.focus: Stage context, nearby alternatives, typography rhythm, and section flow.
lookAround: Does the detail pane indicate where this problem sits in the larger progression?
Do nearby alternatives and stage context help the selected problem feel connected, not isolated?
Does the pane read like curated guidance rather than stacked admin modules?
reviewQuestions: Would I be comfortable reading this panel for more than a few seconds while deciding?
steps.observation: The detail pane should behave like an inspection surface with context, not a box stack.

steps.title: Audit topic-guide integration
steps.intent: Check whether topic-guide entry points feel attached to the same learning system rather than like unrelated utility links.
steps.focus: Relationship between guide shortcuts, selected problem context, and learning routes.
lookAround: Do topic-guide links feel connected to the current route and selected problem?
Are guide shortcuts visible without becoming visually noisy?
Do guide entry points reinforce the field-guide identity?
reviewQuestions: Would I interpret guides as part of the same system rather than extra links?
steps.observation: Topic guides should reinforce the learning map and route logic, not sit as isolated utilities.
